function_v0.doc:
Construct the gateway with the fixed deposit destination (the ramp's
usdc-distributor account) and the USDC Stellar Asset Contract address.
Runs atomically at deploy, so there is no "deployed but uninitialized"
window and nobody can front-run initialization. Both values are
operator-set and immutable: there is no setter and the contract is not
upgradeable, so funds can never be redirected.
function_v0.name: __constructor

function_v0.doc:
Move `amount` USDC from `from` to the fixed destination and emit a
`deposit` event tagged with `order_ref`, atomically.

`from` may be a classic account or a contract (smart-wallet) address.
`from.require_auth()` binds the authorization to the exact invocation \xe2\x80\x94
(from, amount, order_ref) plus a nonce \xe2\x80\x94 so a signed deposit cannot be
replayed or repointed to a different order or amount. The destination is
not a parameter, so funds can never be redirected by the caller.
function_v0.name: deposit

function_v0.doc:
Move `amount` USDC from the fixed destination (usdc-distributor) to `to`
and emit a `payout` event tagged with `order_ref`, atomically \xe2\x80\x94 the
on-ramp leg. `to` may be a contract (smart-wallet) address that a Classic
`Payment` cannot credit.

`destination.require_auth()` means only the usdc-distributor's
authorization can move its funds, so a payout can only be driven by the
ramp itself \xe2\x80\x94 a caller-supplied `to` cannot drain the account. The SAC
transfer's `from` (= destination) auth is covered by the same invocation
tree (and, on-chain, by the source-account credentials when the
usdc-distributor is the transaction source).
function_v0.name: payout

function_v0.doc: The configured USDC Stellar Asset Contract address.
function_v0.name: usdc_sac
